title: Science Synthesis Chamber Behavior Specification
purpose: Define how scientific workstreams are opened, compared, tracked, and archived while preserving provenance, confidence, and consensus state.

chamber_philosophy.principle: Scientific chambers should reduce epistemic drift, not multiply interpretive confusion.
chamber_philosophy.model: A chamber is a bounded scientific context with explicit source classes, method awareness, confidence rules, and continuity anchors.
chamber_philosophy.relationship_to_cycles: Science chambers may span many cycles. Cycles mark operator time; science chambers mark research continuity.

chamber_types.description: Inspect a scientific dataset, schema, scope, provenance, and limits.
Compare multiple studies, papers, or dataset outputs across a shared question.
Monitor emerging findings without overstating maturity.
Maintain stabilized understanding of an established domain.
Translate validated science into human-readable meaning.
examples: human meaning of asteroid catalog update

chamber_types.description: Map scientific findings to public resilience, governance, infrastructure, or health relevance.
examples: sleep dataset relevance to public health
tree-ring climate data and resilience planning

proposed_to_active.trigger: operator starts a focused scientific inquiry
requirements: question defined
domain defined
source class declared

active_to_watching.trigger: evidence remains inconclusive but worth tracking
requirements: frontier classification or insufficient-evidence note
watch cadence defined

watching_to_active.trigger: new data or studies justify renewed analysis
requirements: new source logged
delta from prior state documented

active_to_paused.trigger: missing data, unresolved conflict, or operator pause
requirements: current evidence snapshot
open questions listed
continuity anchors preserved

active_to_closing.trigger: analysis complete or consensus state reached
requirements: consensus state assigned
output artifacts listed
uncertainty boundaries recorded
final synthesis written
sources linked
future review conditions documented
each chamber must declare source class mix
preprints must be explicitly marked
catalog archives must not be treated as causal proof by default
institutional reports must still declare underlying method where possible
confidence must be stated in every chamber close summary
confidence must reflect provenance, method, replication, and cross-source consistency
confidence cannot be raised by rhetorical certainty alone
if metadata is weak, confidence must degrade
every chamber must assign a consensus state before archival
disputed chambers must preserve disagreement explicitly
frontier chambers must include caution against overclaiming
insufficient-evidence chambers must not be narrated as stable findings

continuity_anchors.purpose: Link science work to provenance, prior cycles, and archival memory.

required_anchor_types.description: Which cycle opened the chamber and its active span.
The scientific domain and subdomain this chamber belongs to.
Datasets, studies, or repositories under analysis.
required_anchor_types.example: GWOSC O3 catalog, NASA CNEOS table

required_anchor_types.description: Method classes involved in the chamber.
Which prior consensus archive or canon record this chamber updates or references.

comparison_rules.purpose: Prevent false equivalence or false certainty when comparing science.
requirements: compare like with like where possible
flag methodological mismatch
distinguish sample depth from rhetorical force
do not collapse observational and experimental findings into one bucket
explicitly note when two sources answer different questions

allowed_when: consensus state is assigned or explicitly unknown
must_not_do: turn frontier signals into settled truth
confuse emotional resonance with evidentiary strength

review_required_when: genetic identity or ancestry claims affect populations
scientific findings could materially affect public fear or health behavior
policy recommendation is being drawn from narrow evidence

when_to_spawn: a dataset review becomes a multi-study comparison
a frontier watch needs its own continuity trail
a technical chamber needs a separate Jade translation chamber
a civic implication branch diverges from raw scientific analysis
requirements: new chamber must have a distinct scientific purpose
new chamber must inherit continuity anchors from parent where relevant
spawn event must be logged in parent chamber
translation chambers must not replace the technical chamber

when_to_merge: two chambers converge on the same scientific question
one watch chamber matures into an existing consensus archive
parallel comparisons are duplicating the same synthesis work
requirements: merged chamber must preserve source lineage from both
consensus conflicts must remain visible
merge event must be logged in both chambers

when_to_archive: question sufficiently answered for current cycle
frontier chamber intentionally paused into watch mode archive
merged into a higher-level consensus archive
archive_requirements: review trigger for future re-open documented
anti_drift: do not archive without consensus state
do not archive without provenance anchors
do not silently replace disputed records with cleaner narrative
do not treat readability as grounds to remove uncertainty

example_chamber_template.name: asteroid-risk-observational-synthesis
example_chamber_template.question: How aligned are current public asteroid risk datasets on collision probability and uncertainty framing?
